articles.title: France’s World Cup squad bids adieu to Boston
articles.description:
The team shared a moment with the hotel's staff after more than a month based in the city.

The post France’s World Cup squad bids adieu to Boston appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: France's World Cup team concluded their Boston stay after over a month.
The squad expressed gratitude to hotel staff for their hospitality during their visit.
Boston's local economy benefited from the team's presence, boosting tourism and hotel occupancy.

articles.title: Matt Damon says he’s working on a Karen Read movie
articles.description:
The Massachusetts native said Friday his production company is in the “very early days” of a screen adaptation of Read’s murder case. 

The post Matt Damon says he’s working on a Karen Read movie appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Matt Damon announced plans for a movie about Karen Read's murder case.
The production is in early stages, with no release date set yet.
Local residents may see renewed interest in the controversial case.

articles.title: Bill Belichick’s 1st college team struggled at UNC. He’s hoping lessons learned lead to more success
articles.description:
"Recruiting, fundraising — you name it. There are a lot of different things and I can improve in all of them.”

The post Bill Belichick’s 1st college team struggled at UNC. He’s hoping lessons learned lead to more success appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Bill Belichick reflects on challenges faced by his first college team at UNC.
He emphasizes the need for improvement in recruiting and fundraising efforts.
Lessons learned could influence future success for Boston's football programs.

articles.title: MLB restricts dugout iPad use to prevent use of AI to make decisions
articles.description:
“In many cases, the custom tab had expanded the use of the dugout iPads beyond their originally intended purpose."

The post MLB restricts dugout iPad use to prevent use of AI to make decisions appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: MLB limits dugout iPad use to prevent AI decision-making in games.
New restrictions aim to maintain traditional coaching methods starting July 2026.
Boston teams must adapt strategies as technology use in games is curtailed.

articles.title: Steve Carell and his wife, Nancy, celebrated her birthday at a North End restaurant
articles.description:
Steve Carell and his wife, former "SNL" cast member Nancy Carell (née Walls), celebrated her birthday in the North End this week.

The post Steve Carell and his wife, Nancy, celebrated her birthday at a North End restaurant appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Steve Carell and Nancy Carell celebrated her birthday at a North End restaurant this week.
Nancy Carell, a former 'SNL' cast member, turned another year older during the celebration.
The couple's visit highlights Boston's appeal as a dining destination for celebrities.

articles.title: Some Saugus residents stunned by high water bills, with one nearing $200,000
articles.description:
As the town replaces water meters, about a dozen homeowners received bills with unusually high charges that officials say are under review.

The post Some Saugus residents stunned by high water bills, with one nearing $200,000 appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Saugus residents face unusually high water bills due to meter replacements.
One homeowner received a bill nearing $200,000; officials are reviewing charges.
About a dozen homeowners are affected, raising concerns over billing accuracy.

articles.title: Maine ICE victim relentlessly pursued better life, loved ones say
articles.description:
Karolina Rojas recalled Johan Guerrero, 25, as a tireless worker “who couldn’t stand sitting still,” an adoring partner who treated her like a queen, and a father whose greatest joy was his 3-year-old daughter.

The post Maine ICE victim relentlessly pursued better life, loved ones say appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Johan Guerrero, 25, was a dedicated worker and father from Maine.
He is remembered by partner Karolina Rojas and their 3-year-old daughter.
His story highlights ongoing immigration issues affecting Boston families.
